metadata.summary: VUI market context is reference content for grounding voice-product strategy in current adoption data and platform tradeoffs. It covers voice assistant usage statistics, smart speaker penetration, platform comparison (Alexa, Google Assistant, Siri, Bixby, custom LLM-VUI), and developer access surfaces.

content.title: VUI Market Context
content.summary: VUI market context is reference content for grounding voice-product strategy in current adoption data and platform tradeoffs. It covers voice assistant usage statistics, smart speaker penetration, platform comparison (Alexa, Google Assistant, Siri, Bixby, custom LLM-VUI), and developer access surfaces. Numbers must be stamped with source year and geographic scope — they go stale within 6-12 months.

why.p: Platform selection decisions and stakeholder primers require concrete, sourced adoption figures, not anecdote. The methodology provides a canonical stat schema and a refresh pattern so agents can pull current numbers on demand rather than relying on stale hardcoded values. LLM-native voice agents (ChatGPT Voice, Claude voice) have reshaped the landscape since 2024 and now constitute a fifth platform category bypassing skill stores.

list.item: Strategy phase of a voice-product proposal: ground the deck in current adoption stats and platform tradeoffs.
Platform-selection decision: compare Alexa vs Google vs Siri vs custom LLM-VUI for target geos.
Investor/stakeholder primers: a one-page market context sourced from canonical references.
Quarterly brief refresh: pull current numbers on demand instead of relying on stale README values.
Implementation work — this is descriptive market context, not a how-to methodology.
Platform already locked by contract — re-justification is not useful.
Real-time competitive intelligence — use a market-research methodology with monitoring loops.

rule.statement: Every market statistic must carry four fields: value, year, source URL, and geographic scope. Stats without scope or year are not citable.
rule.rationale: Voice adoption varies 3-5x between US and global figures. A "62% use voice assistants" claim without geographic scope is meaningless and misleads strategy decisions.

rule.statement: Distinguish these three denominator categories: smart-speaker households, voice-assistant users, voice-search query share. These are not interchangeable and come from different measurement methodologies.
rule.rationale: Mixing denominators produces false comparisons. Smart-speaker household penetration tracks device ownership; voice-assistant users tracks intent sessions; voice-search share tracks query-type on a specific engine. Conflating them inflates or deflates the picture arbitrarily.

section.name: current-baseline
section.p: Reference figures as of 2025-2026 (verify and refresh via refresh-script.py before use): 8.4B+ voice assistants in use globally; 20%+ of internet queries use voice search; 62% of US adults use a voice assistant; 55% of US households own a smart speaker; 71% of users prefer voice over typing for some tasks. All figures: US/global mixed — verify geo before reusing.

rule.statement: Stamp every market brief with a refreshed_at date. Refuse to publish if the brief is older than 90 days. Voice adoption statistics moved sharply post-2024 with LLM-native voice agents.
rule.rationale: LLM-native voice agents (ChatGPT Voice, Claude voice mode) are reshaping the landscape faster than canonical 2024-25 sources track. Stats older than 90 days may be wrong on direction, not just magnitude.

section.reference: Python script to refresh market stats via web search

list.item: Statista — primary canonical source for voice adoption stats (some behind paywall)
Voicebot.ai — voice industry tracking, free annual surveys
Edison Research / Infinite Dial — US adoption baselines, annual PDF
Nielsen Norman Group — UX-focused voice interaction analysis
Pew Research — US technology adoption baselines (free)
Gartner — hype cycle and enterprise predictions (paywalled)

rule.statement: Restrict web-search queries to the trusted-source list. Reject SEO content-farm listicles ("10 voice assistant statistics") that cite each other without primary sources.
rule.rationale: LLMs default to ranking high-traffic listicles, which are recycled secondary aggregations. Requiring the agent to return primary-source URLs eliminates circular citation.

section.name: platform-landscape
section.p: Five platforms as of 2025-2026: Alexa (Amazon devices, smart home — developer access via Alexa Skills Kit); Google Assistant (Android, Nest, web — Actions on Google; note: Conversational Actions deprecated 2023); Siri (Apple ecosystem — SiriKit, restricted intent set, requires native iOS app); Bixby (Samsung devices — Bixby Developer Studio, small reach outside Korea); Custom LLM-VUI (any platform via API — OpenAI Realtime, Claude voice, ElevenLabs Conversational — bypasses skill stores and certification entirely).

rule.statement: Weight platform reach by target geo, not global share. Alexa dominates US smart speakers; Google Assistant dominates Android phones globally; Siri dominates iOS. These rankings invert across geos.
rule.rationale: A platform that reaches 40% of US smart-speaker households reaches a very different population than one reaching 40% of Android phones in APAC. Geo-specific reach is the decision-relevant variable, not headline global figures.

rule.statement: Treat LLM-native voice agents as a fifth platform category, not a feature. They bypass skill stores and certification, support open-domain dialog, and have different design requirements (no intent/slot NLU, state managed by model or tool use).
rule.rationale: LLM-native voice agents (Pipecat, Vapi, Retell, Claude voice) represent a different architectural paradigm from intent-based platforms. Conflating them with Alexa/Google design patterns produces broken systems: intent/slot design applied to open LLM dialog, or unconstrained LLM dialog applied where deterministic state is required.

rule.statement: For platform selection: (1) identify target geos and primary user device type; (2) check developer SDK health (commit activity, deprecation notices); (3) assess LLM integration openness; (4) evaluate certification requirements against timeline. Document the tradeoff, not just the winner.
rule.rationale: Platform decisions are long-term bets. A platform with better current reach but a deprecated SDK or a shrinking developer community may be the wrong choice for a two-year product horizon.

template.purpose: Python script: calls Claude with web_search tool to refresh stat list; stamps refreshed_at date.

checklist.check: All market statistics carry four fields: value, year, source URL, geographic scope.
Distinguish between smart-speaker households, voice-assistant users, and voice-search query share.
Market brief is stamped with refreshed_at date.
Brief is less than 90 days old.
Platform reach is weighted by target geo, not global share.
LLM-native voice agents are treated as a distinct platform category.
